blockingIssues.description: agent_specific.mini_swe.model_name uses `aws/anthropic/bedrock-claude-opus-4-6` without a LiteLLM provider prefix. MiniSWE passes model_name directly into litellm.completion(), and LiteLLM rejects raw `aws/...` strings with `BadRequestError: LLM Provider NOT provided`. The same pattern appears in `configs/models/nvidia-bedrock-claude-sonnet-4-6.yaml:17` and `configs/models/nvidia-bedrock-claude-haiku-4-5.yaml:17`, so expected LiteLLM resolution/call behavior is not met.

nonBlockingIssues.description: Worker verification shows NVIDIA endpoint connectivity and authentication, but Claude model test calls returned `team_model_access_denied`, so a successful live completion response was not demonstrated in this environment.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The `python-worker` skill instructs full-repo validators (`uv run pytest -q`, `uv run ruff check .`) which conflicts with mission-scoped validator rules in mission AGENTS.md and led the worker to run noisy full-repo checks before scoping down.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Skill file `/home/ubuntu/git-repos/slop-code-bench/.factory/skills/python-worker/SKILL.md` step 6 vs transcript skeleton commands containing `uv run pytest -q` and `uv run ruff check .`.

commands: uv run python3 - <<'PY' ... from litellm import get_llm_provider ... 'aws/anthropic/bedrock-claude-opus-4-6' ... PY
uv run python3 - <<'PY' ... from minisweagent.models import litellm_model ... inspect.getsource(litellm_model.LitellmModel._query) ... PY
uv run python3 - <<'PY' ... from slop_code.common.llms import ModelCatalog; print(ModelCatalog.get('nvidia/bedrock-claude-opus-4.6')) ... PY

summary: Provider and model YAML files were added as requested, but all three MiniSWE LiteLLM model_name values are currently in a form LiteLLM does not resolve (`aws/...` without provider prefix). This blocks the stated requirement to verify LiteLLM model resolution and successful API calling with these configs.
